prompts.text: Explain to us why people procrastinate on things they care about.
Tell a younger sibling what you wish you'd learned earlier in life.
Tell us which habits are most worth building in your twenties.
Pitch a friend on visiting your hometown.
Argue that it's worth saying sorry first, even when you're not fully wrong.
Tell us what makes a friendship last.
Convince a roommate to start splitting groceries with you.
Convince your manager you should take Friday off.
Explain the signs that a relationship has run its course.
Tell a stranger what they should know before moving to your city.
Make the case that writing things by hand still matters.
Convince a skeptical friend to give therapy a try.
Tell us what people get wrong about confidence.
Imagine you've decided to take a year off from the path everyone expected. Convince your family the timing is right.
Argue that it's okay to outgrow old friendships.
Tell us what's worth spending more money on than most people do.
Argue that boredom is good for you.
You're giving a wedding toast. Tell the crowd what makes the couple work.
Make the case that most people misunderstand what success actually feels like.
Persuade a city council to keep the local library open.
Tell us about a time you changed your mind about something important.
Walk us through the first time you stood up for yourself.
Tell the story of a friendship that surprised you.
Describe the day you realized you needed to leave a job, relationship, or place.
Tell us about a small moment from childhood that shaped how you think today.
Walk us through a time you tried something new and embarrassed yourself.
Tell the story of a trip that didn't go the way you planned.
Describe a conversation that changed your relationship with a family member.
Tell us about the time you almost gave up on something but didn't.
Walk us through the night you finally said the thing you'd been holding back.
Tell the story of someone you misjudged and how you found out.
Describe a moment you got exactly what you wanted and how it actually felt.
Tell us about a time a stranger helped you in a way you'll never forget.
Walk us through the day you lost something that mattered.
Tell the story of a habit you broke and how the breaking actually happened.
Describe a time you trusted your gut over the advice everyone was giving you.
Tell us about a moment you realized your parents were just people.
Walk us through a disagreement that simmered for a long time before it finally came to a head.
Tell the story of a goal you achieved and then immediately questioned.
Describe a time it was hard to forgive someone, and how it finally played out.
Should you take the job that pays more or the one you'd enjoy more?
Is it better to rent or buy in your twenties?
Tell a friend whether they should text their ex back.
Should you go to the wedding of someone you barely talk to anymore?

Is it worth confronting a friend who keeps canceling on you?
Should parents read their teenager's messages?
Is it better to live near family or far from them?

Should you lend money to a close friend who keeps asking?
Is it ever okay to skip a funeral?
Should you keep a friendship that only exists online?

Is it better to plan a wedding small or go all in?
Should you go to the reunion you've been dreading?
Tell a friend whether they should move in with their partner.
Is it worth telling someone a secret that will hurt them?
Should you stay in a city you love for a job you hate?
Imagine you've hosted your family's holidays for years and you're worn out. Tell them whether you'll host again this year.
What's the biggest source of friction in your morning routine and how would you fix it?
Name something that's broken about how friends stay in touch and pitch a fix.
Tell us a problem with how people use their phones at dinner and how to solve it.
What's wrong with how most people pack for trips and what should they do instead?
Identify a problem with how chores get split in shared homes and pitch a fix.
Name what's broken about small talk and propose a better version.

Identify a problem with how people apologize and pitch a real solution.

What's wrong with how most people give advice and what should they do instead?

Tell us what's broken about wedding planning and pitch a solution.

What's wrong with how most people end conversations and what's the fix?

Convince your neighbors to organize a block party.
Persuade a friend who's been isolating to come out this weekend.

Compare growing up in a small town vs a big city.
Contrast spending a Saturday alone vs spending it with friends.
Compare being a morning person vs a night person.
Contrast traveling solo vs traveling with someone you love.
Compare reading a book vs watching the movie version.
Contrast living with roommates vs living alone.
Compare how friendship feels at twenty vs at forty.

Compare being the oldest sibling vs being the youngest.
Contrast a job that pays well vs a job you love.
Compare the version of yourself you show online vs the one your family sees.
Contrast confidence vs arrogance.

Contrast keeping a promise vs breaking one for the right reason.
Compare what your parents wanted for you vs what you actually want.
Contrast forgiving someone vs forgetting what they did.
Compare ambition that comes from joy vs ambition that comes from fear.
Contrast the way grief looks at the start vs how it looks a year in.
Compare freedom you choose vs freedom that's handed to you.
Contrast loving someone the way they want vs loving them the way you know how.
